item.title: Open: This is "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 5, 2026
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Ed O'Keefe speaks to NCAA President Charlie Baker and Reps. Adriano Espaillat and Carlos Gimenez discuss immigration and their American stories.

item.title: NCAA president says he hopes to "dramatically limit" prop betting
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said he hopes to "dramatically limit" prop betting at the collegiate level and beyond.

item.title: Massive funeral for Iran's slain supreme leader enters second day
item.description: The dayslong funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in February by U.S.-Israeli strikes, has been marked by chants for revenge.

item.title: Milions brace for flooding after heat, storms damper July 4th celebrations
item.description: While millions of Americans across the Northeast experienced record-setting temperatures, thunderstorms in the Midwest downed trees, ruptured power lines and made transportation treacherous.

item.title: Transcript: NCAA President Charlie Baker on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 5, 2026
item.description: The following is the full transcript of an interview with NCAA President Charlie Baker, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 5, 2026. This interview was taped on July 1, 2025.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 5)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Nature: Bison on Catalina Island
item.description: We leave you this Sunday among bison grazing on California's Catalina Island. Videographer: Russell Nickerson.

item.title: Full Interview: NCAA President Charlie Baker
item.description: Watch Ed O'Keefe's full interview with NCAA President Charlie Baker, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 5, 2026. Editor's note: This interview was taped on July 1, 2026.

item.title: What would George Washington think of America today?
item.description: Mount Vernon, the home of our nation's first president, is the most-visited historic house in the U.S. But what do Americans visiting Mount Vernon – on the occasion of our country's 250th anniversary – think about what George Washington represents? And today, would the Founding Father recognize the nation he helped create? "Sunday Morning" national correspondent Robert Costa reports.

item.title: Weather woes impact July 4th celebrations as National Mall briefly evacuated
item.description: Americans celebrated 250 years of independence this Fourth of July, although severe weather put a damper on the proceedings in several parts of the country.

item.title: "Little House on the Prairie": The beloved books are back on screen
item.description: Laura Ingalls Wilder's semi-autobiographical book series, which inspired a classic 1970s TV show, is returning to screens this summer in a new adaptation.

item.title: A return to "Little House on the Prairie"
item.description: Laura Ingalls Wilder's semi-autobiographical "Little House on the Prairie" series, which inspired a classic 1970s TV show, is returning to screens this summer in a new adaptation of Wilder's beloved books. Correspondent Faith Salie talks with stars Alice Halsey, Skywalker Hughes, Crosby Fitzgerald and Luke Bracey; writer-showrunner Rebecca Sonnenshine; and executive producer Joy Gorman Wettels, about myth-making and pioneers in the American West.

item.title: Trump holds separate calls with Putin and Zelenskyy on ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump held separate calls with Russia's Vladimir Putin and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy to discuss ending the war.

item.title: Fire up the grill for a yearly ritual: Communing over BBQ skills (or lack thereof)
item.description: Do you have the "right stuff" when it comes to barbecuing? Lots of people believe they do, and they're more than happy to share their wisdom with you, even if unsolicited. Luke Burbank is not one of those people.

item.title: Communing over BBQ skills (or lack thereof)
item.description: Do you have the "right stuff" when it comes to barbecuing? Lots of people believe they do, and they're more than happy to share their wisdom with you, even if unsolicited. Correspondent Luke Burbank is not one of those people.

item.title: J.K. Simmons: A real character
item.description: The Oscar-winning "Whiplash" star talks about being a character actor; his new mob series "The Westies"; and the difficult early days of his career – and an unexpected kindness from an actor friend.

item.title: J.K. Simmons: A real character
item.description: J.K. Simmons, the Oscar-winning star of "Whiplash," relishes being a character actor. He talks with correspondent Tracy Smith about his role as a mob leader in the new MGM+ series "The Westies," and recalls the difficult early days of his career – and how an unexpected kindness from an actor friend helped him when he needed it most.

item.title: Interviewing WWII veterans, the "moral compass of our society"
item.description: Over the past 10 years, Rishi Sharma has conducted video interviews with more than 3,000 World War II veterans – members of the "Greatest Generation" who answered the call to defend freedom – to preserve their stories of sacrifice and their lessons of liberty. Steve Hartman reports.

item.title: Delta Air Lines flight hit by firework while landing at Midway International Airport
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight landing in Chicago was hit by a firework as it was coming to land at Midway International Airport on Saturday.

item.title: Reclaiming the lost art of listening to music
item.description: Digital music gave us the technology, and the freedom, to listen to whatever we want, whenever we want. But more and more people are going back to older, analog ways of listening.

item.title: Reclaiming the lost art of listening to music
item.description: Digital music gave us the technology, and the freedom, to listen to whatever we want, whenever we want. But more and more people are going back to older, analog ways of listening. Correspondent Conor Knighton checks out the Shibuya HiFi bar in Seattle, where curated listening sessions regularly sell out. He also meets artist and engineer Devon Turnbull, whose company, Ojas, designs high-end speakers and listening rooms for public and private spaces.

item.title: Passage: In memoriam
item.description: "Sunday Morning" remembers some of the notable figures who left us recently, including Victor Willis, frontman of the group The Village People.

item.title: The Wedding of the Century: Taylor Swift & Travis Kelce's "mixed marriage"
item.description: Pop superstar Taylor Swift and pro footballer Travis Kelce have tied the knot. Correspondent Mo Rocca talks with Rolling Stone writer Rob Sheffield about the unlikely couple, whose relationship evolved against a backdrop of Swift's songs about romance, heartbreak and marriage. Rocca also talks with Marilyn Monroe biographer Michelle Morgan about another famous union of a media celebrity with a sports legend.

item.title: These United States: Revolutionary writer Thomas Paine
item.description: In 1776, Thomas Paine, an English-born writer shaped by the anti-monarchism movement, wrote "Common Sense," a 47-page pamphlet that changed history, by inspiring American colonists to turn their rebellion against their king into an outright revolution. Correspondent Holly Williams visits the English town of Lewes that helped shaped a future Founding Father and separatist.

item.title: The battle over Stars and Stripes
item.description: The newspaper for the American military has long taken pride in its editorial independence. But under the Trump administration, restrictions have been imposed, and the Pentagon's chief spokesman has vowed to rid the paper of "woke distractions."

item.title: Almanac: July 5
item.description: "Sunday Morning" looks back at historical events on this date.

item.title: Trump touts American "golden age," attacks communism in July 4th speech
item.description: In a speech delayed by a two-hour evacuation of the National Mall due to severe weather, President Trump mixed politics with history.

item.title: CBS' "The Great American Block Party 250" July 4 special features Zac Brown Band, more
item.description: CBS hosts a primetime special celebrating America's 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, with exclusive performances and the largest fireworks show in history.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce were married as they celebrated their wedding with hundreds of guests Friday at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

item.title: 6 presidential speeches that shaped American history
item.description: These six presidential speeches are some that have most reverberated through the ages, and whose impacts are still felt today.
